Nagakura-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町長倉2283番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagakura-jinja}} [[JA:長倉神社 (軽井沢町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852645}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagakura Shrine |画像 = [[File:長倉神社 軽井沢.jpg|250px|View of the precinct (photographed August 20, 2021)]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}{{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451}}{{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064}}{{ill|lt=Nagakura (Karuizawa)|Q107055346|WD=Q107055346|lt=Nagakura}} 2283 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = March 15, August 14 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:長倉神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|Torii (photographed August 20, 2021)]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064}}, {{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'', and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime}} === History === It is located at the east end of {{ill|lt=Kutsukake-shuku|Q3200704|WD=Q3200704}} on the {{ill|lt=Nakasendō|Q1154479|WD=Q1154479}}, northeast of the {{ill|lt=Yukawa (Kitasaku District)|Q11563522|WD=Q11563522|lt=Yukawa River}}. The founding date is unknown, but according to shrine tradition, around the {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138}} era, when Nagakura post station on the old {{ill|lt=Tōsandō|Q1334011|WD=Q1334011}} was developed, it was enshrined on Mount Nagakura, and the former site was called “Moto-Hachiman.” Kutsukake-shuku stood about 400 m south of the current site until its relocation after a fire in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1773|Q7692|WD=Q7692}}), and Nagakura Shrine is presumed to have moved at the same timeTanigawa (2000) p.224. The oldest stone structure in the precinct is a stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963}}), comparatively recent, supporting relocation with the post-town move. There is also a poetry monument to Kutsukake Tokijirō by {{ill|lt=Shin Hasegawa|Q11653726|WD=Q11653726}}. An alternative theory identifies Nagakura Suwa Shrine in Miyota, Miyota-machi (same district), where {{ill|lt=Oitai-shuku|Q3357559|WD=Q3357559}} was located, as the Shikinaisha “Nagakura Shrine”Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia p.577. === Notes === === References === * ''Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia'', {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11501456|WD=Q11501456|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.}}, 1983 * {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}} (ed.), ''Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano'', 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:軽井沢町の建築物]] [[Category:軽井沢町の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagakura Shrine |画像 = [[File:長倉神社 軽井沢.jpg|250px|View of the precinct (photographed August 20, 2021)]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Nagakura (Karuizawa)|Q107055346|WD=Q107055346|lt=Nagakura|1=Nagakura}} 2283 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = March 15, August 14 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:長倉神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|Torii (photographed August 20, 2021)]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} === History === It is located at the east end of {{ill|lt=Kutsukake-shuku|Q3200704|WD=Q3200704|1=Kutsukake-shuku}} on the {{ill|lt=Nakasendō|Q1154479|WD=Q1154479|1=Nakasendō}}, northeast of the {{ill|lt=Yukawa (Kitasaku District)|Q11563522|WD=Q11563522|lt=Yukawa River|1=Yugawa River}}. The founding date is unknown, but according to shrine tradition, around the {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} era, when Nagakura post station on the old {{ill|lt=Tōsandō|Q1334011|WD=Q1334011|1=Tōsandō}} was developed, it was enshrined on Mount Nagakura, and the former site was called “Moto-Hachiman.” Kutsukake-shuku stood about 400 m south of the current site until its relocation after a fire in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1773|Q7692|WD=Q7692|1=1773}}), and Nagakura Shrine is presumed to have moved at the same timeTanigawa (2000) p.224. The oldest stone structure in the precinct is a stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}}), comparatively recent, supporting relocation with the post-town move. There is also a poetry monument to Kutsukake Tokijirō by {{ill|lt=Shin Hasegawa|Q11653726|WD=Q11653726|1=Shin Hasegawa}}. An alternative theory identifies Nagakura Suwa Shrine in Miyota, Miyota-machi (same district), where {{ill|lt=Oitai-shuku|Q3357559|WD=Q3357559|1=Otai-shuku}} was located, as the Shikinaisha “Nagakura Shrine”Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia p.577. === Notes === === References === * ''Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia'', {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11501456|WD=Q11501456|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.}}, 1983 * {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.), ''Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano'', 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:軽井沢町の建築物]] [[Category:軽井沢町の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== located in or next to body of water (P206) == * {{ill|Yu River|lt=Yu River|ja|湯川 (北佐久郡)|WD=Q11563522|1=Yugawa River}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6100005003841 == Commons category (P373) == * Nagakura-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町長倉2283番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagakura-jinja}} [[JA:長倉神社 (軽井沢町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852645}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagakura Shrine |画像 = [[File:長倉神社 軽井沢.jpg|250px|View of the precinct (photographed August 20, 2021)]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Nagakura (Karuizawa)|Q107055346|WD=Q107055346|lt=Nagakura|1=Nagakura}} 2283 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = March 15, August 14 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:長倉神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|Torii (photographed August 20, 2021)]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} === History === It is located at the east end of {{ill|lt=Kutsukake-shuku|Q3200704|WD=Q3200704|1=Kutsukake-shuku}} on the {{ill|lt=Nakasendō|Q1154479|WD=Q1154479|1=Nakasendō}}, northeast of the {{ill|lt=Yukawa (Kitasaku District)|Q11563522|WD=Q11563522|lt=Yukawa River|1=Yugawa River}}. The founding date is unknown, but according to shrine tradition, around the {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} era, when Nagakura post station on the old {{ill|lt=Tōsandō|Q1334011|WD=Q1334011|1=Tōsandō}} was developed, it was enshrined on Mount Nagakura, and the former site was called “Moto-Hachiman.” Kutsukake-shuku stood about 400 m south of the current site until its relocation after a fire in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1773|Q7692|WD=Q7692|1=1773}}), and Nagakura Shrine is presumed to have moved at the same timeTanigawa (2000) p.224. The oldest stone structure in the precinct is a stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}}), comparatively recent, supporting relocation with the post-town move. There is also a poetry monument to Kutsukake Tokijirō by {{ill|lt=Shin Hasegawa|Q11653726|WD=Q11653726|1=Shin Hasegawa}}. An alternative theory identifies Nagakura Suwa Shrine in Miyota, Miyota-machi (same district), where {{ill|lt=Oitai-shuku|Q3357559|WD=Q3357559|1=Otai-shuku}} was located, as the Shikinaisha “Nagakura Shrine”Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia p.577. === Notes === === References === * ''Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia'', {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11501456|WD=Q11501456|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.}}, 1983 * {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.), ''Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano'', 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:軽井沢町の建築物]] [[Category:軽井沢町の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== located in or next to body of water (P206) == * {{ill|Yu River|lt=Yu River|ja|湯川 (北佐久郡)|WD=Q11563522|1=Yugawa River}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6100005003841 == Commons category (P373) == * Nagakura-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町長倉2283番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagakura-jinja}} [[JA:長倉神社 (軽井沢町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852645}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagakura Shrine |画像 = [[File:長倉神社 軽井沢.jpg|250px|View of the precinct (photographed August 20, 2021)]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Nagakura (Karuizawa)|Q107055346|WD=Q107055346|lt=Nagakura|1=Nagakura}} 2283 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = March 15, August 14 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:長倉神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|Torii (photographed August 20, 2021)]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} === History === It is located at the east end of {{ill|lt=Kutsukake-shuku|Q3200704|WD=Q3200704|1=Kutsukake-shuku}} on the {{ill|lt=Nakasendō|Q1154479|WD=Q1154479|1=Nakasendō}}, northeast of the {{ill|lt=Yukawa (Kitasaku District)|Q11563522|WD=Q11563522|lt=Yukawa River|1=Yugawa River}}. The founding date is unknown, but according to shrine tradition, around the {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} era, when Nagakura post station on the old {{ill|lt=Tōsandō|Q1334011|WD=Q1334011|1=Tōsandō}} was developed, it was enshrined on Mount Nagakura, and the former site was called “Moto-Hachiman.” Kutsukake-shuku stood about 400 m south of the current site until its relocation after a fire in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1773|Q7692|WD=Q7692|1=1773}}), and Nagakura Shrine is presumed to have moved at the same timeTanigawa (2000) p.224. The oldest stone structure in the precinct is a stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}}), comparatively recent, supporting relocation with the post-town move. There is also a poetry monument to Kutsukake Tokijirō by {{ill|lt=Shin Hasegawa|Q11653726|WD=Q11653726|1=Shin Hasegawa}}. An alternative theory identifies Nagakura Suwa Shrine in Miyota, Miyota-machi (same district), where {{ill|lt=Oitai-shuku|Q3357559|WD=Q3357559|1=Otai-shuku}} was located, as the Shikinaisha “Nagakura Shrine”Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia p.577. === Notes === === References === * ''Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia'', {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11501456|WD=Q11501456|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.}}, 1983 * {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.), ''Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano'', 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:軽井沢町の建築物]] [[Category:軽井沢町の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

== located in or next to body of water (P206) == * {{ill|Yu River|lt=Yu River|ja|湯川 (北佐久郡)|WD=Q11563522|1=Yugawa River}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6100005003841 == Commons category (P373) == * Nagakura-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町長倉2283番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagakura-jinja}} [[JA:長倉神社 (軽井沢町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852645}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagakura Shrine |画像 = [[File:長倉神社 軽井沢.jpg|250px|View of the precinct (photographed August 20, 2021)]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Nagakura (Karuizawa)|Q107055346|WD=Q107055346|lt=Nagakura|1=Nagakura}} 2283 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = March 15, August 14 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:長倉神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|Torii (photographed August 20, 2021)]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} === History === It is located at the east end of {{ill|lt=Kutsukake-shuku|Q3200704|WD=Q3200704|1=Kutsukake-shuku}} on the {{ill|lt=Nakasendō|Q1154479|WD=Q1154479|1=Nakasendō}}, northeast of the {{ill|lt=Yukawa (Kitasaku District)|Q11563522|WD=Q11563522|lt=Yukawa River|1=Yugawa River}}. The founding date is unknown, but according to shrine tradition, around the {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} era, when Nagakura post station on the old {{ill|lt=Tōsandō|Q1334011|WD=Q1334011|1=Tōsandō}} was developed, it was enshrined on Mount Nagakura, and the former site was called “Moto-Hachiman.” Kutsukake-shuku stood about 400 m south of the current site until its relocation after a fire in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1773|Q7692|WD=Q7692|1=1773}}), and Nagakura Shrine is presumed to have moved at the same timeTanigawa (2000) p.224. The oldest stone structure in the precinct is a stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}}), comparatively recent, supporting relocation with the post-town move. There is also a poetry monument to Kutsukake Tokijirō by {{ill|lt=Shin Hasegawa|Q11653726|WD=Q11653726|1=Shin Hasegawa}}. An alternative theory identifies Nagakura Suwa Shrine in Miyota, Miyota-machi (same district), where {{ill|lt=Oitai-shuku|Q3357559|WD=Q3357559|1=Otai-shuku}} was located, as the Shikinaisha “Nagakura Shrine”Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia p.577. === Notes === === References === * ''Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia'', {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11501456|WD=Q11501456|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.}}, 1983 * {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.), ''Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano'', 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:軽井沢町の建築物]] [[Category:軽井沢町の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

== located in or next to body of water (P206) == * {{ill|Yu River|lt=Yu River|ja|湯川 (北佐久郡)|WD=Q11563522|1=Yugawa River}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6100005003841 == Commons category (P373) == * Nagakura-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町長倉2283番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagakura-jinja}} [[JA:長倉神社 (軽井沢町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852645}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagakura Shrine |画像 = [[File:長倉神社 軽井沢.jpg|250px|View of the precinct (photographed August 20, 2021)]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Nagakura (Karuizawa)|Q107055346|WD=Q107055346|lt=Nagakura|1=Nagakura}} 2283 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = March 15, August 14 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:長倉神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|Torii (photographed August 20, 2021)]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} === History === It is located at the east end of {{ill|lt=Kutsukake-shuku|Q3200704|WD=Q3200704|1=Kutsukake-shuku}} on the {{ill|lt=Nakasendō|Q1154479|WD=Q1154479|1=Nakasendō}}, northeast of the {{ill|lt=Yukawa (Kitasaku District)|Q11563522|WD=Q11563522|lt=Yukawa River|1=Yugawa River}}. The founding date is unknown, but according to shrine tradition, around the {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} era, when Nagakura post station on the old {{ill|lt=Tōsandō|Q1334011|WD=Q1334011|1=Tōsandō}} was developed, it was enshrined on Mount Nagakura, and the former site was called “Moto-Hachiman.” Kutsukake-shuku stood about 400 m south of the current site until its relocation after a fire in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1773|Q7692|WD=Q7692|1=1773}}), and Nagakura Shrine is presumed to have moved at the same timeTanigawa (2000) p.224. The oldest stone structure in the precinct is a stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}}), comparatively recent, supporting relocation with the post-town move. There is also a poetry monument to Kutsukake Tokijirō by {{ill|lt=Shin Hasegawa|Q11653726|WD=Q11653726|1=Shin Hasegawa}}. An alternative theory identifies Nagakura Suwa Shrine in Miyota, Miyota-machi (same district), where {{ill|lt=Oitai-shuku|Q3357559|WD=Q3357559|1=Otai-shuku}} was located, as the Shikinaisha “Nagakura Shrine”Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia p.577. === Notes === === References === * ''Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia'', {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11501456|WD=Q11501456|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.}}, 1983 * {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.), ''Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano'', 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:軽井沢町の建築物]] [[Category:軽井沢町の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

== located in or next to body of water (P206) == * {{ill|Yu River|lt=Yu River|ja|湯川 (北佐久郡)|WD=Q11563522|1=Yugawa River}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6100005003841 == Commons category (P373) == * Nagakura-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町長倉2283番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagakura-jinja}} [[JA:長倉神社 (軽井沢町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852645}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagakura Shrine |画像 = [[File:長倉神社 軽井沢.jpg|250px|View of the precinct (photographed August 20, 2021)]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Nagakura (Karuizawa)|Q107055346|WD=Q107055346|lt=Nagakura|1=Nagakura}} 2283 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = March 15, August 14 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:長倉神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|Torii (photographed August 20, 2021)]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} === History === It is located at the east end of {{ill|lt=Kutsukake-shuku|Q3200704|WD=Q3200704|1=Kutsukake-shuku}} on the {{ill|lt=Nakasendō|Q1154479|WD=Q1154479|1=Nakasendō}}, northeast of the {{ill|lt=Yukawa (Kitasaku District)|Q11563522|WD=Q11563522|lt=Yukawa River|1=Yugawa River}}. The founding date is unknown, but according to shrine tradition, around the {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} era, when Nagakura post station on the old {{ill|lt=Tōsandō|Q1334011|WD=Q1334011|1=Tōsandō}} was developed, it was enshrined on Mount Nagakura, and the former site was called “Moto-Hachiman.” Kutsukake-shuku stood about 400 m south of the current site until its relocation after a fire in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1773|Q7692|WD=Q7692|1=1773}}), and Nagakura Shrine is presumed to have moved at the same timeTanigawa (2000) p.224. The oldest stone structure in the precinct is a stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}}), comparatively recent, supporting relocation with the post-town move. There is also a poetry monument to Kutsukake Tokijirō by {{ill|lt=Shin Hasegawa|Q11653726|WD=Q11653726|1=Shin Hasegawa}}. An alternative theory identifies Nagakura Suwa Shrine in Miyota, Miyota-machi (same district), where {{ill|lt=Oitai-shuku|Q3357559|WD=Q3357559|1=Otai-shuku}} was located, as the Shikinaisha “Nagakura Shrine”Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia p.577. === Notes === === References === * ''Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia'', {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11501456|WD=Q11501456|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.}}, 1983 * {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.), ''Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano'', 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:軽井沢町の建築物]] [[Category:軽井沢町の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

== located in or next to body of water (P206) == * {{ill|Yu River|lt=Yu River|ja|湯川 (北佐久郡)|WD=Q11563522|1=Yugawa River}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6100005003841 == Commons category (P373) == * Nagakura-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町長倉2283番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagakura-jinja}} [[JA:長倉神社 (軽井沢町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852645}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagakura Shrine |画像 = [[File:長倉神社 軽井沢.jpg|250px|View of the precinct (photographed August 20, 2021)]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Nagakura (Karuizawa)|Q107055346|WD=Q107055346|lt=Nagakura|1=Nagakura}} 2283 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = March 15, August 14 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:長倉神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|Torii (photographed August 20, 2021)]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} === History === It is located at the east end of {{ill|lt=Kutsukake-shuku|Q3200704|WD=Q3200704|1=Kutsukake-shuku}} on the {{ill|lt=Nakasendō|Q1154479|WD=Q1154479|1=Nakasendō}}, northeast of the {{ill|lt=Yukawa (Kitasaku District)|Q11563522|WD=Q11563522|lt=Yukawa River|1=Yugawa River}}. The founding date is unknown, but according to shrine tradition, around the {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} era, when Nagakura post station on the old {{ill|lt=Tōsandō|Q1334011|WD=Q1334011|1=Tōsandō}} was developed, it was enshrined on Mount Nagakura, and the former site was called “Moto-Hachiman.” Kutsukake-shuku stood about 400 m south of the current site until its relocation after a fire in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1773|Q7692|WD=Q7692|1=1773}}), and Nagakura Shrine is presumed to have moved at the same timeTanigawa (2000) p.224. The oldest stone structure in the precinct is a stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}}), comparatively recent, supporting relocation with the post-town move. There is also a poetry monument to Kutsukake Tokijirō by {{ill|lt=Shin Hasegawa|Q11653726|WD=Q11653726|1=Shin Hasegawa}}. An alternative theory identifies Nagakura Suwa Shrine in Miyota, Miyota-machi (same district), where {{ill|lt=Oitai-shuku|Q3357559|WD=Q3357559|1=Otai-shuku}} was located, as the Shikinaisha “Nagakura Shrine”Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia p.577. === Notes === === References === * ''Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia'', {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11501456|WD=Q11501456|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.}}, 1983 * {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.), ''Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano'', 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:軽井沢町の建築物]] [[Category:軽井沢町の歴史]]

== located in or next to body of water (P206) == * {{ill|Yu River|lt=Yu River|ja|湯川 (北佐久郡)|WD=Q11563522|1=Yugawa River}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6100005003841 == Commons category (P373) == * Nagakura-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町長倉2283番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagakura-jinja}} [[JA:長倉神社 (軽井沢町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852645}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagakura Shrine |画像 = [[File:長倉神社 軽井沢.jpg|250px|View of the precinct (photographed August 20, 2021)]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Nagakura (Karuizawa)|Q107055346|WD=Q107055346|lt=Nagakura|1=Nagakura}} 2283 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = March 15, August 14 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:長倉神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|Torii (photographed August 20, 2021)]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|WD=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|WD=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} === History === It is located at the east end of {{ill|lt=Kutsukake-shuku|Q3200704|WD=Q3200704|1=Kutsukake-shuku}} on the {{ill|lt=Nakasendō|Q1154479|WD=Q1154479|1=Nakasendō}}, northeast of the {{ill|lt=Yukawa (Kitasaku District)|Q11563522|WD=Q11563522|lt=Yukawa River|1=Yugawa River}}. The founding date is unknown, but according to shrine tradition, around the {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} era, when Nagakura post station on the old {{ill|lt=Tōsandō|Q1334011|WD=Q1334011|1=Tōsandō}} was developed, it was enshrined on Mount Nagakura, and the former site was called “Moto-Hachiman.” Kutsukake-shuku stood about 400 m south of the current site until its relocation after a fire in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1773|Q7692|WD=Q7692|1=1773}}), and Nagakura Shrine is presumed to have moved at the same timeTanigawa (2000) p.224. The oldest stone structure in the precinct is a stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}}), comparatively recent, supporting relocation with the post-town move. There is also a poetry monument to Kutsukake Tokijirō by {{ill|lt=Shin Hasegawa|Q11653726|WD=Q11653726|1=Shin Hasegawa}}. An alternative theory identifies Nagakura Suwa Shrine in Miyota, Miyota-machi (same district), where {{ill|lt=Oitai-shuku|Q3357559|WD=Q3357559|1=Otai-shuku}} was located, as the Shikinaisha “Nagakura Shrine”Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia p.577. === Notes === === References === * ''Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia'', {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11501456|WD=Q11501456|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.}}, 1983 * {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.), ''Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano'', 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:軽井沢町の建築物]] [[Category:軽井沢町の歴史]]

== located in or next to body of water (P206) == * {{ill|Yu River|lt=Yu River|ja|湯川 (北佐久郡)|qid=Q11563522|1=Yugawa River}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6100005003841 == Commons category (P373) == * Nagakura-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町長倉2283番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagakura-jinja}} [[JA:長倉神社 (軽井沢町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852645}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagakura Shrine |画像 = [[File:長倉神社 軽井沢.jpg|250px|View of the precinct (photographed August 20, 2021)]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|qid=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|qid=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Nagakura (Karuizawa)|Q107055346|qid=Q107055346|lt=Nagakura|1=Nagakura}} 2283 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|qid=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = March 15, August 14 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:長倉神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|Torii (photographed August 20, 2021)]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Karuizawa|Q1012064|qid=Q1012064|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Kitasaku District|Q1142451|qid=Q1142451|1=Kitasaku District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|qid=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime}} === History === It is located at the east end of {{ill|lt=Kutsukake-shuku|Q3200704|qid=Q3200704|1=Kutsukake-shuku}} on the {{ill|lt=Nakasendō|Q1154479|qid=Q1154479|1=Nakasendō}}, northeast of the {{ill|lt=Yukawa (Kitasaku District)|Q11563522|qid=Q11563522|lt=Yukawa River|1=Yugawa River}}. The founding date is unknown, but according to shrine tradition, around the {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|qid=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} era, when Nagakura post station on the old {{ill|lt=Tōsandō|Q1334011|qid=Q1334011|1=Tōsandō}} was developed, it was enshrined on Mount Nagakura, and the former site was called “Moto-Hachiman.” Kutsukake-shuku stood about 400 m south of the current site until its relocation after a fire in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1773|Q7692|qid=Q7692|1=1773}}), and Nagakura Shrine is presumed to have moved at the same timeTanigawa (2000) p.224. The oldest stone structure in the precinct is a stone lantern from {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|qid=Q6963|1=1731}}), comparatively recent, supporting relocation with the post-town move. There is also a poetry monument to Kutsukake Tokijirō by {{ill|lt=Shin Hasegawa|Q11653726|qid=Q11653726|1=Shin Hasegawa}}. An alternative theory identifies Nagakura Suwa Shrine in Miyota, Miyota-machi (same district), where {{ill|lt=Oitai-shuku|Q3357559|qid=Q3357559|1=Otai-shuku}} was located, as the Shikinaisha “Nagakura Shrine”Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia p.577. === Notes === === References === * ''Nagano Prefecture Encyclopedia'', {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11501456|qid=Q11501456|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Development Bureau Publishing Dept.}}, 1983 * {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.), ''Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano'', 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:軽井沢町の建築物]] [[Category:軽井沢町の歴史]]
